arts.ttl: Girona fear season-ending knee injury for Tottenham Hotspur loanee Bryan Gil
arts.sum: It has not been a good season for Girona, whose struggles continued on Saturday with a disappointing draw at home to Valencia. To make matters worse, one of their relied-upon attackers could have played his final match for the club. Bryan Gil missed the match against Valencia, and post-match, Girona head coach Michel Sanchez confirmed […] The post Girona fear season-ending knee injury for Tottenham Hotspur loanee Bryan Gil appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Tottenham confirmed team vs Fulham - Seven changes with Archie Gray in midfield
arts.sum: Vicario; Spence, Romero, Davies, Udogie; Bentancur, Bissouma, Gray; Johnson, Solanke, Tel. Subs: Kinsky; Porro, Van de Ven, Bergvall, Sarr, Maddison, Odobert, Son, Scarlett. Postecoglou has decided that a string of players can't be risked with such a short turnaround so while Cristian Romero and Djed Spence start, Micky van de Ven is on the bench alongside Thursday's matchwinner Wilson Odobert as well as Son Heung-min, James Maddison and Lucas Bergvall, who suffered from cramp at the end of the game.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou launches into remarkable defence against criticism of his Tottenham tenure
arts.sum: I’ve said many times it doesn’t concern me because I’m not really sure how people perceive this role and particularly me as a person, that they would think I would worry about tomorrow if there’s a negative outcome," he said. However, when Postecoglou, who won the Asian Cup as manager of Australia, was asked whether he thinks the scrutiny focused on him is more intense because of his background in coaching across the world, winning league titles back home and in Japan, before doing the same in Scotland at Celtic, rather than coming through the European game, it opened the floodgates to an impassioned and lengthy monologue to the small band of reporters in the room.
